informal_statement.text: Given a slice of integers, return a new vector containing the same multiset of elements in nondecreasing order.
preconditions: the input is a slice of i32 values of any length
required_properties: the output length equals the input length
the output is sorted in nondecreasing order
the output is a permutation of the input (same multiset of elements)
edge_cases: slice of length that is a power of two
slice of length that is not a power of two

semantic_units.description: Length preservation: the output has the same length as the input.
common_failure_modes: stating only a length bound instead of equality

semantic_units.description: Sortedness: the output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
Permutation: the output is a permutation of the input (equal multiset of elements).
Stability is NOT required by the spec.

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output length equals the input length.
The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
The output is a permutation of the input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem spec_sort (xs : Arr) :
  Sorted (mergeSort xs) ∧ IsPerm (mergeSort xs) xs := by
  exact ⟨by
    simpa [Sorted, mergeSort] using CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.MergeSortTheory.mergeSort_sorted xs,
    by
    simpa [IsPerm, IsPermutation, mergeSort] using CTA.Benchmark.Sorting.MergeSortTheory.mergeSort_perm xs⟩
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Introduce the specification context for sorting a list of integers.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int), ∃ (ys : Vector Int xs.length), True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The result is a vector of integers whose length matches the input length.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int) (ys : Vector Int xs.length), ys.length = xs.length
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output vector has the same length as the input slice.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int) (ys : Vector Int xs.length), (∀ i j : Fin xs.length, i ≤ j → ys[i] ≤ ys[j]) → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output is in nondecreasing order (indices respect ≤).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int) (ys : Vector Int xs.length), (ys.toList ~ xs) → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output contains exactly the same multiset of elements as the input (permutation).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (xs : List Int), ∃ (ys : Vector Int xs.length), (∀ i j : Fin xs.length, i ≤ j → ys[i] ≤ ys[j]) ∧ (ys.toList ~ xs)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists an output vector of the same length that is nondecreasing and is a permutation of the input.
